disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Bilateral malar rash sparing the nasolabial folds; afebrile; no spreading induration/pus/systemic toxicity (against cellulitis/erysipelas)
trigger_question_examples: Is the rash on both cheeks and does it skip the creases beside your nose?
Any fever, spreading hot swelling, or pus?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's on both cheeks and skips the creases beside my nose, no fever, no spreading swelling or pus.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No pleuritic chest pain/breathlessness (serositis) and no cola urine/ankle swelling (nephritis) — no organ crisis
trigger_question_examples: Any sharp chest pain on breathing or breathlessness?
Any cola/red urine, less urine, or swollen ankles?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No chest pain on breathing or breathlessness, and my urine and ankles are normal.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sun-triggered burning erythema confined to sun-exposed 'butterfly' areas, on a background of positive ANA
trigger_question_examples: Did it come up after being in the sun, and is it just on the sun-exposed cheeks/nose?
Do you have a known ANA/autoimmune condition tendency?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, it came up after the sun on my cheeks and nose, and I have a positive ANA.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Does a face rash mean my organs are being attacked? Do I need hospital?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Screen serositis/nephritis; if negative, explain a sun-triggered a sun-sensitive autoimmune skin condition (malar) rash is a skin flare that does not mean organ involvement, advise cool compress + mild topical steroid + strict photoprotection, and give an organ-crisis safety-net — without ED over-triage.
